title: T-001 — Lock 4 warehouse enums (lp_status / lp_qa_status / item_type_snapshot / lp_source) (WH §5.2, §6.1, §6.2)
prompt:
# T-001 — Lock 4 warehouse enums (lp_status / lp_qa_status / item_type_snapshot / lp_source) (WH §5.2, §6.1, §6.2)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §5.2, §6.1, §6.2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Lock the four core warehouse Postgres enums + matching TypeScript unions so every downstream table, migration, service, and Drizzle query shares an identical value set (ADR-001 LP-as-only-inventory baseline).

## Implementation contract
1. Create `packages/db/src/schema/warehouse-enums.ts` with `pgEnum` declarations for: lp_status = ['available','reserved','consumed','shipped','blocked','merged']; lp_qa_status = ['PENDING','PASSED','FAILED','HOLD','RELEASED','QUARANTINED','COND_APPROVED']; item_type_snapshot = ['rm','intermediate','fg','co_product','byproduct']; lp_source = ['grn','wo_output','split','merge','adjustment'].
2. Emit Drizzle migration `0NN_warehouse_enums.sql` creating the four types via `CREATE TYPE ... AS ENUM (...)` in dependency-free order (no FKs yet).
3. Export typed string-literal unions (`LpStatus`, `LpQaStatus`, `ItemTypeSnapshot`, `LpSource`) and `ALL_LP_STATUSES` etc. as `as const` tuples for runtime guards.
4. Add runtime `isLpStatus(v: string): v is LpStatus` predicates used by services in later tasks.

## Files
- packages/db/src/schema/warehouse-enums.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/0NN_warehouse_enums.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/warehouse-enums.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs against an empty DB, when `\dT lp_status` is queried, then it returns exactly 6 values matching PRD §5.2 in the same order.
2. Given a consumer imports `LpQaStatus`, when they assign 'passed' (lowercase) or any value outside the 7-state set, then TypeScript raises a type error at compile time.
3. Given the runtime guard `isLpStatus('merged')` runs, when validated against lp_status, then it returns true; given `isLpStatus('archived')` runs, then it returns false.
4. Given the migration is applied twice, when re-run, then it is idempotent (or guarded with `DROP TYPE IF EXISTS` only when the migration is regenerated).

## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/db/src/schema/warehouse-enums.test.ts` asserting (a) each tuple length, (b) presence of every PRD §5.2 value, (c) `assertNever`-style exhaustiveness for `LpStatus` switch.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test warehouse-enums` and capture results in CLOSEOUT.

## Risk red lines
- Do not add stray enum values not in PRD §5.2.
- Do not lowercase qa_status (UPPER_SNAKE is the wire format).
- Do not create any tables, columns, or FK references in this migration — enums only.
- Do not collapse `item_type_snapshot` enum into `item_type`; the snapshot variant is ADR-002 critical.
